response_text:
:::writing
To: Department Heads  
From: Finance and Administration Office  
Subject: Temporary Budget Freeze – Immediate Implementation  
Date: [Insert Date]

Due to current revenue variability and pending reimbursement adjustments, the hospital is implementing a temporary budget freeze effective immediately. This step is intended to stabilize operating expenses while leadership reviews financial projections for the coming quarters. Patient care and safety remain our highest priorities, and this measure is designed to minimize disruption to essential services.

What is affected:
• New non‑essential equipment purchases not already approved and ordered  
• Initiation of new vendor contracts or service agreements  
• Non‑critical facility upgrades or renovations  
• Discretionary spending such as travel, conferences, and non‑mandatory training  
• Hiring for non‑clinical or non‑critical vacant positions unless specifically approved by executive leadership

What is not affected:
• Direct patient care supplies and medications  
• Staffing required for patient safety, including clinical roles and urgent backfills  
• Previously approved capital purchases that have already been processed or ordered  
• Compliance, regulatory, or safety-related expenditures  
• Maintenance or repairs necessary to keep clinical operations functioning

What we need from department managers:
Please review your current spending plans and identify any upcoming purchases, hiring requests, or contract renewals that may be affected by the freeze. If you believe a paused item is essential to patient care, regulatory compliance, or operational continuity, submit a brief justification to Finance for review.

Managers should also bring forward:
• Any anticipated supply shortages or operational risks created by delaying purchases  
• Critical staffing gaps that could impact patient care or safety  
• Time‑sensitive vendor renewals or service agreements  
• Projects already underway that cannot be paused without significant cost or disruption

Finance and Administration will review exception requests on a case‑by‑case basis in coordination with hospital leadership.

This freeze is intended to be temporary. We will provide an update once financial conditions are reassessed and additional guidance is available.

Thank you for your cooperation and for continuing to prioritize safe, high‑quality patient care during this period.
:::
